Title: PRpnp, a novel dual activity PNP family protein improves plant vigour and confers multiple stress tolerance in Citrus aurantifolia

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: purine metabolites support growth and metabolism; trypsin inhibition reduces insect nutrient utilization
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: maintains intracellular purine reservoir and improves plant vigour; additionally confers insect resistance through trypsin inhibitory activity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PRpnp localizes to both nucleus and cytosol in Nicotiana benthamiana transient assays. 2. Overexpressed PRpnp in Citrus aurantifolia retained both PNP enzymatic activity and trypsin inhibitory activity. 3. PRpnp transgenics showed improved vigour, broad abiotic stress tolerance, and pest resistance.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PNP family protein / PNP-UDP domain disrupted by 46-aa insert with trypsin inhibitory loop
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: salt; drought; oxidative stress; alkaline pH; Papilio demoleus; Scirtothrips citri
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: subcellular localization assay; transgenic overexpression; PNP activity assay; trypsin inhibitory assay; qRT-PCR; western blot; abiotic stress and insect bioassays
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper's sole experimentally central gene is PRpnp. Many ABA/JA pathway genes were differentially expressed but were not directly functionally tested as core genes in this study.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable candidate for citrus improvement for vigour and combined abiotic/biotic stress resistance

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: active cytokinin promoting plant vigour and growth/development
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: cytokinin interconversion among CK bases, ribosides and nucleotides
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: purine nucleosides; cytokinin ribosides/nucleotides (inferred interconversion role)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: purine bases; increased CK free base tZ
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: functions as a purine salvage enzyme maintaining intracellular purine reservoir and is proposed to promote cytokinin interconversion leading to increased active CK free base trans-zeatin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool / required for interconversion that increases active target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: increases endogenous CK free base tZ accumulation in transgenic Citrus aurantifolia
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PRpnp overexpression in Citrus aurantifolia increased endogenous trans-zeatin (~2.1-fold) under normal conditions. 2. The gene functions as a purine salvage PNP-family enzyme and is proposed to regulate cytokinin interconversion/homeostasis. 3. PRpnp shows dual activity, retaining PNP activity while also possessing trypsin inhibitory activity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: constitutively overexpressed in transgenic leaves/plants under 2x35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PNP/UDP family protein with a 46-aa insert containing trypsin inhibitory loop
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: normal growth conditions for CK quantification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression in Citrus aurantifolia; PNP activity assay; LC-MS/MS cytokinin quantification; RNA-seq support
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: no obvious growth penalty reported; instead improved plant vigour
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper infers CK interconversion activity from purine salvage function and increased tZ levels; direct enzymatic CK interconversion assay was not shown. PRpnp is also neofunctionalized as a trypsin inhibitor.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful candidate for engineering improved plant vigour together with multi-stress tolerance; may be combined with stress-resilience traits while monitoring hormone balance

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA and JA mediate abiotic and biotic stress tolerance and defense signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: metabolic/signaling regulator with dual enzyme-protease inhibitor activity
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis/signaling of ABA and JA; hormone homeostasis; defense responses
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive regulation of endogenous hormone accumulation, likely indirect via transcriptional reprogramming
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: AAO3; ABA3; PYL9; ABCG40; BZIP16; BZIP60; AREB3; NAC52; BHLH130; LOX6; OPR3; AOC3; 4CLL5/OPCL1; 4CLL7; 4CLL9; KAT2; WRKY75; PLA1; PP2C51 (downregulated)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: upregulates multiple ABA- and JA-biosynthesis/signaling genes and downregulates negative regulator PP2C51
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes endogenous ABA (~1.86-fold) and JA (~2.56-fold) accumulation, enhancing tolerance to salt, drought, oxidative and alkaline stress and improving resistance to Papilio demoleus and Scirtothrips citri
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-independent inducer that elevates basal ABA and JA levels and thereby confers multi-stress resistance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PRpnp overexpression caused broad transcriptomic activation of ABA- and JA-related genes in Citrus aurantifolia under control conditions. 2. LC-MS/MS validated significantly higher endogenous ABA and JA in transgenic plants. 3. Elevated ABA and JA are proposed to act synergistically to improve tolerance to multiple abiotic stresses and contribute to insect resistance, especially JA-mediated thrips resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: constitutively overexpressed in transgenic plants under 2x35S promoter
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PNP/UDP family protein with 46-aa insert and trypsin inhibitory loop
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: synergistic interaction between elevated ABA and JA is proposed; direct feedback loop not established
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: normal growth conditions for RNA-seq and hormone profiling; salt, drought, oxidative stress, alkaline pH, Papilio demoleus feeding, and Scirtothrips citri feeding for phenotype interpretation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression; RNA-seq differential expression analysis; LC-MS/MS hormone profiling; stress and insect bioassays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: no penalty reported; improved vigour observed
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong candidate for engineering constitutive broad-spectrum stress resilience through endogenous hormone elevation, though hormone-related pleiotropy should be monitored
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PRpnp is not a canonical transcription factor; its regulatory effect on ABA/JA appears indirect through metabolic or signaling changes associated with purine homeostasis and dual trypsin inhibitor function.
